user_query:
I’m 70 and have lived in Grantsville my whole life. Bill and I go to the same LDS ward, and I’d like a small, repeatable script that pulls recent official Newsroom articles from the Church’s site and summarizes how often our local interests are mentioned. Please write a script (place it at scripts/newsroom_harvest.py) that:

1) Uses the official Church Newsroom site (domain pattern: newsroom.churchofjesuschrist.org). Programmatically fetch robots.txt from that domain, parse the Sitemap lines, and download the first sitemap XML that appears to list article pages on that same domain (if there are multiple, prefer one whose URLs contain “/article”; if none, use the first sitemap listed). Save these raw files to:
- data/raw/robots.txt
- data/raw/sitemap.xml

2) From the downloaded sitemap, collect up to the first 20 unique article URLs under the same domain (prefer URLs containing “/article”; if fewer than 20 match, fill the remainder from other URLs in the sitemap, still under the same domain). Download each page’s HTML and save to data/pages/ using a safe, filesystem-friendly filename derived from the URL (e.g., data/pages/<slug>.html).

3) Extract the main article information for each downloaded page and produce a structured record per article with fields: url (string), title (string), published_date (if available; ISO 8601 string or null), word_count (integer based on extracted main text), and keyword_counts (object mapping each keyword to its count in the article text, case-insensitive). The list of keywords is provided locally at input/keywords.txt (one keyword per line). Write one JSON Lines file with one record per article at data/parsed/articles.jsonl.

4) Compute summary aggregates and save to:
- data/summary/summary.json containing: total_articles (integer), total_words (integer), average_words_per_article (number), articles_per_year (object year->count based on published_date year where available; otherwise count under a key “unknown”), and aggregate_keyword_counts (object keyword->total occurrences summed across all articles).
- data/summary/keyword_counts.csv with two columns: keyword,count (headers included), where count equals the totals from aggregate_keyword_counts.

5) Write a small consistency file at data/summary/checks.json that includes: feed_candidates_count (number of URLs discovered in the chosen sitemap), pages_downloaded_count, parsed_records_count, totals_match (boolean asserting pages_downloaded_count == parsed_records_count == summary.total_articles), and the exact keyword list read from input/keywords.txt.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not hardcode specific article URLs. The script must discover them via robots.txt -> sitemap -> URLs under the same domain.
- Limit processing to the first 20 discovered URLs following the preference rules above to keep runtime modest.
- If an article’s published date cannot be parsed from the page or feed metadata, set published_date to null and count it under “unknown” in articles_per_year.
- Make sure all the output paths exist and are written exactly as specified above.

Input: input/keywords.txt (provided here in the workspace). Outputs: all artifacts under data/raw/, data/pages/, data/parsed/, and data/summary/ as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
